entry.title: Why Bringing SAP to Agentic SDK via Regular MCP Server Is Not Good Enough
entry.content:
Advocating Technology

Let me name things properly – we have lots of noise around AI. Companies are trying to jump into the technology because they think that Business AI is simple, that it is just a kind of ChatGPT that gets access to business data. Sounds simple, doesn’t it?

I strongly disagree! Bringing SAP data to an Agentic SDK, like Claude or SAP Joule, does not mean delivering Business AI. It is far away from even an understanding of business needs. I know, you start hating me 😊. And the only reason for the internal opinion in your head is that you are most probably one of the MCP bridge providers that think that wrapping APIs (I will not write “discover OData via MCP Server,” because the whole SAP community will hate me :D), or wrapping SAP tables/views, or wrapping SAP function modules is the way to bring SAP data to Business AI. 

How many posts do we see now on LinkedIn where people are happy like kids seeing data from an agentic SDK, e.g. Claude Desktop, returned by OData that is called via an MCP Server and shown as a table. Wow-wow! A list of Purchase Orders! As a table! Based on my utterance! C’mon people, we are not in 2017 when we, together with the SAP Conversational AI team, were happy to see such kinds of cases. Yes, 9 years ago it was innovation, not now. Why not post on LinkedIn that the same OData service draws an even nicer table in a standard SAP Fiori app showing a list of Purchase Orders with… OMG… DRILL DOWN!!! 😊

Why am I writing this? Just to emphasize that Business AI is an excellent technology that is now far beyond strange posts where people see SAP data in another UI than GUI or Fiori. Real Business AI returns investment, works autonomously, and understands data. For example, an AI agent that gets emails from customers with purchase orders from their systems confirming the deals we’ve made with them. That AI agent posts those emails to SAP, creating Sales Orders, for sure not using OData. Why not? Because there are two EntitySet calls you need to do to create a Sales Order via OData, and you need to be able to simulate a batch call; otherwise, you will get lots of Sales Order headers in your SAP productive system when the OData call for Sales Order Items has failed.

 Pic.1 Example from LinkedIn Article – Creating Sales Order via OData without Batch Mode

However, even creating a Sales Order from my example is not the biggest challenge for that AI agent.

The Biggest Challenge

SAP data and the “bridges” we build to connect AI agents with SAP via “regular” MCP servers, this is the biggest challenge we create for AI agents. Sometimes it seems that after allocating a really good business case, we are doing everything possible to push the AI agent to fail 😊.

SAP data preparation is the boring, but extremely essential, step here. We should think about what we feed to the AI agent when asking it to read an email and post a Sales Order in the SAP system. We need to give the AI agent a chance to query master data that is so essential for that AI automation scenario and allocate master data in a robust and reliable way, independent of how the customer names our services and products in their systems.

SAP Master Data Vectorization

Let’s see a simple example we have created using the ChatGPT UI showing how different AI agents will act if you prepare and only then bridge SAP data properly. We will use two MCP servers for this:

  • Regular MCP Server for SAP Data (any MCP server, we do not like to point fingers at someone’s solution; we’d like to advocate AI automation only)

  • Skybuffer AI MCP Server for SAP Data (here we have a data preparation layer where we vectorize SAP master data first)

 Pic.2 SAP Master Data Vectorization as a Data Preparation Step

See the Difference

Now let’s simulate a request from an AI agent that is working autonomously and has responsibility to create Sales Orders in an SAP production system with the maximum level of automation.

Here is a simple example of a regular MCP Server failure, just because the customer captured data in their system a bit differently from our SAP productive system:

Pic.3 Regular MCP Server for SAP Data – Failing on the Simple Case of Small Wording Differences

And now let’s see how easily and robustly an AI agent gets information from the same SAP system, but via an MCP Server that reads prepared SAP master data for AI automation:

 Pic.4 Skybuffer AI MCP Server – Easy and Robust Access to SAP Master Data

Now you see that 99% of all examples or success stories from LinkedIn about how fast we can create MCP servers to bridge SAP data and how reusable OData services are, are solutions that are far away from productive implementation (unless you are OK with getting a 50% automation rate 😊).

Conclusion

Indeed, MCP servers that consume OData, tables/views, etc. make sense for some steps in AI automation. However, we need to be very careful, and we should not mix hype with productive implementations. A good MCP server bridges prepared SAP data that an AI agent can consume to provide a maximal rate of automation that will reach 100% in a short time of productive usage. Saving on implementation ruins the impression of Business AI and reduces trust in it. Let’s not harm our overall revenue and investment return, and think twice whether the bridge we build for an AI agent suits the goal.

Imagine connecting the Skybuffer AI MCP Server to SAP Joule, making it able to understand user input by similarities. How powerful would your SAP Joule implementation be, and how smoothly would SAP Joule understand requests? Skybuffer AI runs on SAP BTP, providing a hidden but necessary layer for SAP Joule that can properly bridge your SAP on-premise data.

Closing Statement

Good luck with AI automation implementations, and do not hesitate to ask questions, collaborate, and bring technology to customers in a way they benefit from it, not just seeing the hype in it.

entry.title: Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management in SAP Cloud ERP 2602
entry.content:
Welcome to your one-stop shop to learn and share knowledge about SAP's ready-to-run public cloud ERP software solution.  

SAP Cloud ERP is part of SAP Business Suite that brings AI, data, and applications together into a fully integrated system. This community is open for anyone in the Cloud ERP ecosystem–customers, partners, and SAP experts.  

 

In this blog post I’d like to outline the innovations for SAP Cloud ERP 2602 in the area of Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management. These are the topics that are covered:

  • Enhanced integration of project plan information with SAP Cloud ERP (6JN) 
  • Project staffing view for resource managers (6JO) 
  • Allowing contingent workers (cost center procured) to record time on enterprise projects (1NT, 1Q4) 
  • Automatic removal of billing block for sales order billing plans (1NT) 
  • Joule as project assistant (beta), planned to be available mid-March
  • Further innovations

 

Check out my video to get a glimpse of the new features:

 

In today's fast-paced business environment, running projects efficiently, on time, and on budget is more critical than ever. This release is packed with features designed to bring more intelligence, automation, and transparency to your projects, ultimately driving significant business value. We’ve focused on streamlining processes, enhancing integration, and even giving you a glimpse into the future with AI-powered assistance.

Let's dive into the highlights that will help you unlock new levels of project success.

 

Enhancements in SAP Project and Resource Management

 

Enhanced integration of project plan information with SAP Cloud ERP

In complex projects spanning engineering, manufacturing, and services, a major challenge is keeping project execution plans in sync with core financial and logistical data. A delay in a design phase, for example, has a direct impact on procurement timelines and billing dates. If this information isn't shared instantly between the project planning tool and the core ERP, decisions are made based on outdated information. To solve this, our 2602 release supercharges the integration between SAP Project and Resource Management, our solution on the SAP Business Technology Platform, and SAP Cloud ERP. Now, when a project manager adjusts a project plan – such as extending a phase – the system automatically synchronizes these date changes with the core ERP in real time.

This direct synchronization ensures that your financial, commercial, and logistical data within SAP Cloud ERP always reflects the most current project reality, empowering you to make reliable decisions based on a single source of truth. By automating the update process, you eliminate the need for project teams to manually replicate date changes across systems, which significantly reduces administrative effort and minimizes the risk of human error. This tight integration also lays the groundwork for faster billing cycles, as milestone completions in the project plan can now directly trigger commercial processes in the core system.

 

Figure 1: Improved integration of project plan information between SAP Cloud ERP and SAP Project and Resource Management, enables better decision-making in projects through reliable, up-to-date project plan information.

 

Project staffing view for resource managers

Resource managers are constantly juggling demands from multiple projects, and gaining a clear, consolidated overview of the staffing situation for a specific project can be a fragmented and time-consuming process. To address this pain point, we are introducing a brand-new SAP Fiori app called “Manage Request Assignments”. This powerful application is designed specifically for resource managers, providing a single, project-centric view of all resource requests. It shows at a glance which roles have been fully staffed, which are partially staffed, and which are still open, eliminating the need to dig through multiple screens and reports.

This new application provides resource managers with unprecedented transparency into the staffing health of any project, allowing them to instantly identify bottlenecks and open demands without extensive research. Having all the necessary information in one place, with direct navigation to take action, streamlines the workflow and dramatically reduces the time it takes to staff open requests and get the right people on the right tasks. Ultimately, a clear, holistic overview empowers resource managers to more effectively optimize staffing within a single project or re-allocate experts across multiple projects, ensuring your most valuable resources are deployed with maximum impact.

 

Figure 2: The “Manage Request Assignments” application allows users to efficiently view the resource request and assignment details at a project level.

 

Other Innovations in SAP Project and Resource Management

Additional enhancements introduced in SAP Project and Resource Management. For example:

  • Technical user flow coverage (client credential flow) for some APIs
  • Use of actual dates in scheduling
  • Consideration of working and none working days in scheduling
  • Ability to block certain file types during file upload
  • Additional details for tasks and collaboration project

For a complete list and more details please take a look into the What's new document for SAP Project and Resource Management

 

 

Enhancements in Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management

 

Allowing contingent workers (cost center procured) to record time on enterprise projects

Modern project teams are a dynamic blend of internal employees and external contingent workers. A significant process gap existed for contingent workers who were procured via a cost center, as they couldn't use the standard “My Timesheet” application to record time against enterprise projects. This made it difficult to get a complete and timely picture of all labor hours and costs. With the 2602 release, we have closed this gap. Now, these contingent workers can use the very same “My Timesheet” application as internal employees to record their time against the projects they are staffed on, creating a unified experience for the entire team.

This enhancement delivers a unified and simplified process where all project resources, regardless of their employment type, use a single application for time recording, which improves the user experience. You can now capture every hour worked against your project demands in one central place, providing a more accurate and timely view of total project costs and progress. Furthermore, the system automates the creation of the corresponding service entry sheet and goods receipt upon timesheet approval, significantly reducing administrative overhead and ensuring your suppliers are paid accurately and on time.

 

Figure 3: Project-based and cost center-based contingent workers can record time seamlessly in the “Manage My Timesheet” application, ensuring consistent tracking and better visibility across all engagement types.

 

Automatic removal of billing block for sales order billing plans

Milestone billing is a fantastic way to align payments with project progress, but it has often been hampered by a manual step. Once a milestone was achieved, someone had to go into the sales order and manually remove the billing block to make that item available for invoicing, a delay that directly impacts cash flow. We have now fully automated this process. Building on a feature from our last release, the system now automatically removes the billing block from the corresponding billing plan item in the related sales order the moment a project manager confirms the completion of that milestone in the project.

By completely automating this process, you eliminate the manual hand-off and inherent delays, allowing you to invoice your customers the very moment a milestone is contractually met. This directly accelerates your order-to-cash cycle and improves cash flow. This automation also removes a tedious and error-prone manual task, freeing up your project controllers and sales administrators to focus their expertise on more strategic, value-added activities. The risk of human error is entirely eliminated, leading to a smoother, more reliable, and more accurate billing process from end to end.

 

Figure 4: For sales order billing plan items which are created based on enterprise project or WBS element milestones, the billing block is automatically removed when the related milestone is completed.

 

Joule as project assistant (beta), planned to be available mid-March

Setting up a new project can be a complex and time-consuming task, often requiring project managers to navigate between three or four different applications to define the scope, staff resources, and release the project for time recording. We are incredibly excited to give you a preview of how we are solving this with the future of project management: the Joule Project Assistant. This is our generative AI copilot, and we are starting with a beta agent designed to radically simplify the creation of internal projects. Instead of clicking through multiple screens, a project manager can simply ask Joule in natural language, e.g. "Create a new pre-sales project for the 'Rising Star' initiative, starting next week and lasting for one month.". Joule can then propose a template, adjust dates, staff the project, and release it – all within a single, conversational session.

This AI-powered assistant promises dramatic efficiency gains, condensing a multi-application process into a single, swift conversation that saves significant time and effort for your project managers. The intuitive, conversational nature of Joule makes project creation accessible to everyone, drastically reducing the learning curve and empowering users to initiate projects quickly and correctly. By leveraging templates from successful past projects, the Joule agent also helps ensure that new projects are set up consistently and in compliance with your company's standards right from the start. We are currently running a beta program for this and encourage you to get involved! Register for the Customer Engagement Initiative via this landing page as of March 15, 2026.

 

Figure 5: The AI-powered solution assists users in rapidly setting up projects by leveraging similar past projects when the volume is high, and projects are repetitive in nature.



Other innovations in Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management

We know how critical it is to have a clear, unified view of project financials. That's why we’ve introduced the company code currency into the Project Cost report. Now, you can see a consolidated view of all your key cost figures including planned costs, actuals, commitments, and even project stock costs – all in a single, consistent currency. This provides a much clearer picture of your project's overall financial health and variance.

There is one important prerequisite to keep in mind: for the report to display in the company code currency, all the projects and WBS elements you select for that report must share the same company code. This ensures data integrity and accurate reporting. For a more detailed guide on this feature, I encourage you to check out the documentation on the SAP Help Portal.

Second, we’ve Improved system usability by segregating the profitability segment from settlement rules for clearer allocation and analysis. What does this mean for you? In the past, assigning profitability might have required you to set up a settlement rule. Now, for projects with revenue, you can assign the profitability segment directly to a billing WBS element using the "Assign" function. This is a much more direct and intuitive workflow. It saves you steps, simplifies the allocation process, and makes your analysis clearer and more efficient. As with all our features, you can find comprehensive details on this enhancement in the SAP Help Portal.

Finally, and this is something I’m personally very excited about, we are going beyond system features to build a stronger community. We are officially introducing the new CIP community – a collaborative space designed for our customers, partners, and SAP experts to connect. The goal is to build a powerful network where we can all share ideas, discuss best practices, and co-innovate on the future of enterprise and collaborative project scenarios. This is your platform to influence the direction of our products and learn from your peers.

If you are interested in joining this community and helping shape the future of project management at SAP, please reach out to my colleagues, Anke Junker anke.junker@sap.com or Varsha Chetan Manu varsha.chetan.manu@sap.com, to get involved.


The SAP Cloud ERP 2602 release for EPPM is all about making your project management more connected, automated, and intelligent. From ensuring a single source of truth to simplifying daily tasks for every project role, these innovations are designed to deliver tangible business value and set you up for success.

Thank you for your time, and I look forward to seeing how you leverage these new capabilities.

 

 

📽️ Watch the replays of our SAP Cloud ERP 2602 Early Release Series!

From January 12-16, we hosted a series of 22 expert-led live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP Cloud ERP 2602 release. Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. You will find the recording and presentation for Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management as well as 21 other topics of interest. Don't miss this chance and watch them on demand – anywhere, anytime.

 

🔎Find all our assets

We at Cloud ERP and SCM Product Success offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

entry.title: Product Compliance in SAP Cloud ERP 2602
entry.content:
Welcome to your one-stop shop to learn and share knowledge about SAP's ready-to-run public cloud ERP software solution.  

 SAP Cloud ERP is part of SAP Business Suite that brings AI, data, and applications together into a fully integrated system. This community is open for anyone in the cloud ERP ecosystem–customers, partners, and SAP experts.  

 

In this blog post I’d like to outline the innovations for SAP Cloud ERP 2602 Release in the area of Product Compliance. These are the topics that are covered:

Safety Data Sheet and Label Management

  • Extension of safety data sheet (SDS) generation (7DO)
  • SDS management and shipment with branded products with specific data for final SDS generation (3VR)
  • Definition of a fallback SDS for a product (3VQ)

 Dangerous Goods Management

  • Support for multi-component products that contain two or more dangerous goods (3FC)

 

In case you do not need all the information below and briefly want to get an overview of the release highlights, please watch this video with the top four key innovations:

Video#1: SAP Cloud ERP 2602 for Product Compliance

 

Extension of safety data sheet (SDS) generation



Business Background
To maintain compliance, it is crucial that Safety Data Sheets (SDS) reflect the latest regulatory requirements and clearly communicate any updates to recipients. This includes providing more granular information in specific sections, such as handling and storage, and highlighting changes between document versions for better transparency.

Key Features
This innovation improves the SDS generation process for regulatory assessments, incorporating updated requirements for Section 7 (Handling and Storage) and Section 15 (Regulatory Information). It is now possible to include vertical change marks in the final SDS, which visually indicate all data that has been modified since the last released version, ensuring changes are easily identifiable. Additionally, the GHS classification has been enhanced to allow for the maintenance of the index number and annex name, which will be included as output on the SDS.

 

 

Video#2: Demo Extension of safety data sheet (SDS) generation



SDS management and shipment with branded products with specific data for final SDS generation



Business Background
Companies often market the same base product under different brand names, each with unique compliance obligations for the specific markets in which they are sold. It is essential to manage market-specific data, such as Poison Centre Notification (PCN) details like the Unique Formula Identifier (UFI), and ensure this information is correctly represented on the SDS for that branded product.

Key Features
This innovation allows you to apply specific compliance purposes to branded products, determining which data is relevant for the markets where the product is sold. The system now supports compliance requirement versions for the Poison Centre Notification, enabling you to manage the registration status and UFI number for a branded product. When an SDS is generated and shipped, this UFI number is now automatically included on the final document.

 


Video#3: Demo SDS management and shipment with branded products with specific data for final SDS generation

 

Definition of a fallback SDS for a product



Business Background
In scenarios where a product is shipped to a country without a specific, locally compliant SDS being available, the shipment process could previously fail, causing delays. To prevent these interruptions, businesses need a contingency to provide a standard, alternative SDS, ensuring that at least some safety information is shared with the customer and the shipment can proceed.

Key Features
You can now define a global fallback SDS within the system by activating it for a specific compliance requirement, such as "German SDS in English." If the system does not find a released, country-specific SDS for a product during shipment, it automatically uses this predefined fallback document. This feature helps to minimize shipment errors and ensures that customers receive critical safety information, even if it is not in the 100% correct local format.

 


Video#4: Demo Definition of a fallback SDS for a product

 

Support for multi-component products that contain two or more dangerous goods



Business Background
Products that are sold as a single unit but consist of multiple components (e.g., a two-part glue kit) present a challenge for dangerous goods management. Classifying each component individually is often insufficient, as regulations may require classifying the entire kit as a single entity to ensure compliant handling, checks, and documentation throughout the logistics process.

Key Features
This feature integrates the processing of multi-component products directly into the existing dangerous goods framework, allowing for classification at the product header level. Users can now classify the entire multi-component product, define packaging details for the kit, and assign relevant enclosure variants for transport. This ensures that dangerous goods checks in sales and delivery processes are executed correctly on the main product, not the individual components.

 

 

Video#5: Support for multi-component products that contain two or more dangerous goods

 

Thanks for taking the time to read this blog.

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts. 

 

More Information

For a more in-depth exploration, please note that the EHS & Product Compliance Infodays will be held in Amsterdam from March 24th to March 25th. You can view the agenda and register here.


️Watch the replays of our SAP Cloud ERP 2602 Early Release Series! 

From January 12 to January 16, 2026, we hosted a series of 22 expert-led live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the 2602 release of SAP Cloud ERP. Don't miss this chance and watch them on demand–anywhere, anytime. 

 

Find all our assets

entry.title: Release Highlights SAP Integrated Product Development Q1, 2026
entry.content:
New year, new features! Welcome to our blog series on the latest developments in the SAP Cloud ERP 2602 release. This blog highlights four innovations in SAP Integrated Product Development, our ready-to-run Cloud PLM software solution.  We are starting the year strong, adding more cloud-native technologies that help you manage your business processes end-to-end, for innovation and sustainable products. 

In this blog post, I’d like to outline some of the latest features of SAP Integrated Product Development, from releases 2509 through 2602. These are the topics that are covered:

  • Defining documents for components of the product structure

  • Defining customer-specific properties for product structure instances

  • AI-assisted hotspot creation for visualization diagrams

  • Dynamic data visualization

In case you do not need all the details below and briefly want to get an overview of the release highlights, please watch this video to get a high-level glimpse on these innovations:

Video#1: Release Highlights SAP Integrated Product Development

What's New in Enterprise Product Structure?

Defining documents for components of the product structure

Business Background

Thanks to the integration of document management features into the product structure of SAP IPD, users can now work with documents with more ease, directly from their product structures. This new feature enables users to use a document as describing entity in the Enterprise Product Structure (EPS). ​ They can link documents to a selected level of the product structure and access documents directly from the structure.

Key Features

Search for documents and link them to a selected level of the product structure. Control the validity of different documents, for example, by linking a specific document to a specific product series. Also, access existing documents directly from the Enterprise Product Structure.

 

Video#2: Defining documents for components of the product structure

 

Defining customer-specific properties for product structure instances

Business Background

This new feature enables customers to specify customer-specific instance properties with values and units of measure, increasing flexibility and supporting subsequent processes by defined properties and values on instances within product structures. Components automatically inherit the correct set of properties based on their assigned product category. This ensures consistent data, reduces manual setup, and makes it easy to standardize attributes across similar products.

Key Features

Specify product properties with values and units of measure, add map them to instances of the product structure. You can use this feature, for example, to map properties from CAD files via design items to products and product structures.

 

Video#3: Defining customer-specific properties for product structure instances

 

And what are new highlights of Visualization?

Now let’s have a look at our latest innovations for Visualization, including the following AI-powered feature:

AI-assisted hotspot creation

Business Background

You can now automatically generate hotspots from 2D drawings, including piping and instrumentation diagram (P&ID) symbols, and associate them with back-end business data. This significantly improves the efficiency of hotspot creation and business data mapping and helps to reduce human errors resulting from manual processes. Use this innovation to digitalize PDF drawings for asset management and streamline the digital transformation journey of legacy drawings.

Key Features

Use AI to automatically create hotspots from 2D drawings and associate them with back-end business data. The system uses a trained object detection model to recognize piping and instrumentation diagram (P&ID) symbols and automatically create hotspots for the detected symbols. Then, it lets you map the generated hotspots with the relevant business data. 

 

Video#4: AI-assisted hotspot creation

 

Dynamic data visualization

Business Background

This new feature enables you to remotely monitor key systems or operations (such as the production line or asset) in real time. Display real-time data (for example, sensor or IoT data) in visualizations to reflect live temperature, pressure, and so on. You can monitor changes to the data, spotting issues as they emerge and empowering them to react instantly to minimize downtime.  

Key Features

Visualize changes to key parameters in real time.  Administrators can create templates to determine the styling and color of the data to be displayed, and other details of the data mapping. Users can choose a styling, data mapping, and refresh time of the content based on such templates.

 

Video#5: Dynamic data visualization

 

If you want to learn more about the latest highlights on Manufacturing in SAP Cloud ERP 2602, read this blog post.

Watch the replays of our SAP Cloud ERP 2602 Early Release Series! 

From January 12-16, 2026, we hosted a series of compelling live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP Cloud ERP 2602 release. Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. You can find the recordings of your past sessions on SAP Learning. As a partner, you will find the recording and presentation for Manufacturing as well as other topics of interest via the Empowering SAP partners site.

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page on ERP or our community page on PLM to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts. 

Follow me on LinkedIn and stay up-to-date with our latest enablement assets: linkedin.com/in/annette-garcia-lorenzo

 Find all our assets  

The release 2602 showcases more 130 innovation highlights across the portfolio. Check out our release document for all the details!

We at Cloud ERP and SCM Product Success offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Click on the enablement wheel below and check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you:

entry.title: Manufacturing in SAP Cloud ERP 2602
entry.content:
Hello and welcome to the latest SAP Cloud ERP 2602 release. We are kicking off the year with a significant wave of enhancement in the area of manufacturing and it would be great if you join the ride!  Annette and I are happy to guide you through the latest enhancements and innovations. First things first, here is the release highlight video: 

Video#1: Release Highlights Overview

 

Production Engineering 

New Features for Production Engineering, including AI

We are proud to introduce our new AI-assisted production routing proposal and copy routing feature. Production engineers and master data specialists know that creating new production routings involves a lot of repetitive data entry, especially when you are creating data for similar products. To solve this issue and speed up the process, we are introducing AI-assisted production routing proposals. 

AI-assisted production routing proposal and copy routing feature

The AI engine analyzes existing production routings to find similar routings based on material name, description, and bill of material. Instead of starting from scratch, users can copy and adapt the most relevant routing. This innovation directly translates into significant time savings, a lower risk of data entry errors, and greater consistency across your master data. It helps your teams work faster and smarter. 

This demo was given during our Manufacturing Early Release Webinar: 

 

Video#2: Demo AI-assisted production routing proposal and copy routing

​As a prerequisite, you need to enable the related ISLM scenario. For information on setting up the ISLM scenario, see this blog post.

New Configuration Activity

Additionally, we are introducing a new configuration activity for the Responsible Capacity Planner Group. Use the new configuration activity Determine Capacity Planning to define the person or group of people who are responsible for planning capacities.

New configuration activity Determine Capacity Planning

If you want to find out about what is coming for product lifecycle management in the Cloud, please refer to this blog post on SAP Integrated Product Development.

 

New Features for Production Planning & Execution, including AI  

The next innovations are particularly relevant for production planners and manufacturing supervisors who are looking to streamline their daily operations, gain deeper insights, and leverage the power of automation and AI. Lets explore the key highlights that will help you optimize your end-to-end processes.


Manage Material Coverage - Navigation to Related Apps

The Manage Material Coverage app has been enhanced to include additional navigation options. These new links provide you with fast and easy access to detailed information on materials and MRP elements directly from your worklist.

 

Manage Material Coverage - Navigation to Related Apps

This enhancement helps material planners and MRP controllers to quickly analyze situations and make informed decisions without navigating through multiple screens, which significantly streamlines the material analysis process.

To help you analyze and resolve coverage issues more quickly, navigation options were added for the following MRP elements:

Receipt Elements:

  • Purchase Order
  • Purchase Requisition
  • Planned Order
  • Stock Transport Order
  • Stock Transport Requisition
  • Stock

Requirement Elements:

  • Sales Order
  • Delivery
  • Order Reservation
  • Dependent Requirement
  • Planned Independent Requirement
  • Stock Transfer Order Release
  • Stock Transfer Requisition Release

This demo was given during our Manufacturing Early Release Webinar: 

 

Video#3: Manage Material Coverage - Navigation to Related Apps


Capacity Scheduling Table

With this feature, you can now view a "Missing Components" popup by clicking the Missing Components icon within the Capacity Scheduling Table.

Capacity Scheduling Table 

This provides production planners and schedulers with immediate visibility into material shortages that could impact the production schedule. This direct access to critical information allows for quicker problem resolution and more resilient production planning.

Manage Work Center Capacity

The following enhancements were done with the latest CFDs and also the 2602 main release: 

  • 2508.1: View the Customer name and ID through the Customer column. You can view this column by clicking the Settings wheel in the Items table in the Operations section.
  • 2508.1: View the Consolidated Shift Capacity and the Remaining Capacity. These columns can be viewed by clicking the Table icon in the Overview section.
  • 2602: View the Utilization in the form of a line micro chart instead of a bar micro chart when you load the app. This enables you to visualize data for a maximum of 750 data points based on the Evaluation Horizon instead of the 28 days limit for the bar micro chart.

Manage Work Center Capacity

For further details and demo you can navigate to this 2508.1 blog post: Enhancing Manufacturing Excellence: What's New in ... - SAP Community

Review Order Progress - Order Selection and Views

In the Review Order Progress - Multilevel View app, the filtering options for order numbers have been improved. Now, depending on the document type you select, different filter fields are provided as mandatory to ensure the order number you specify matches the document type.

Review Order Progress - Order Selection and Views

Furthermore, you can now create and save personalized views of the order components table, allowing you to access your preferred layout and settings quickly.

Check out this demo from our 2602 Manufacturing Early Release Webinar: 

 

Video#4: Review Order Progress - Order Selection and Views

External Scheduling for Planned Orders

This feature enables you to update your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system with schedule data from an external scheduler. This is crucial for organizations that use specialized, third-party tools for detailed production scheduling. It ensures that data remains consistent between your planning tools and your system of record, providing a single source of truth for your production plan.


Manage Production Orders - Production Planning and Operations Agent

This feature is currently in the BETA testing phase, which is ending soon. The general availability (GA) for all customers will be officially announced shortly. This feature introduces the ability to start the Production Planning and Operations Agent directly from the worklist of the Manage Production Orders app. A new "Release" button is now available, enabling you to release your production orders assisted by agentic AI technology. This innovation helps production supervisors automate and optimize the order release process, improving efficiency and reducing manual workload.

Manage Production Orders - Production Planning and Operations Agent

What can the agent do for you so far: 

  • Assisting the production planners and supervisors in releasing up to 20 production orders at a time.
  • The agent automates prerequisite checks necessary for releasing production orders, including component availability and alternatives as well as work center capacity checks.
  • The agent also proposes rescheduling when the prerequisites for release are not met.
  • Once all criteria are met, the planner can confirm the proposed order changes, and the agent will complete the production order release.

This feature will not automatically be available in your system but requires that you activate the feature Release with Production Planning and Operations Agent  (PP_FIO_SFC_AI297_RELEASEAGENT) in the configuration activity Activate Features (if you are in the BETA testing program). 

Watch this demo that was given during the 2602 Manufacturing Early Release Webinar:

 

Video#5: Manage Production Orders - Production Planning and Operations Agent

Working with AI-assisted features like this is a key part of our innovation strategy. Please note that to access this generative AI feature, an additional entitlement may be required once this feature will be delivered to all customers. Before enabling such features, ensure you adhere to your company's compliance framework regarding the usage of generative AI. You should also acknowledge that the AI-Assisted Smart summarization does not provide the technical capabilities to support the collection, processing, and storage of personal data. Please consult your account executive for more information. Stay tuned for more information soon on this topic. 

Combined Order Enablement 

A significant efficiency improvement is now available for discrete manufacturing. This feature enables you to combine identical operations from different production orders into a single, consolidated production order. The combination can include either individual operations or entire orders.

Combined Order Enablement

This process, often known as order combination, helps reduce setup times and manufacturing costs by processing similar requirements together. This innovation runs under the new scope item 7TO. 

This demo was presented during the 2602 Manufacturing Early Release Webinar: 

 

Video#6: Combined Order Enablement 

Feel free to follow us in the SAP community and on LinkedIn to not miss any updates.

Watch the replays of our SAP Cloud ERP 2602 Early Release Series! 

From January 12-16, 2026, we hosted a series of compelling live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP Cloud ERP 2602 product release. Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you.  You can find the recordings of your past sessions on SAP Learning. As a partner, you will find the recording and presentation for Manufacturing as well as other topics of interest via the Empowering SAP partners site.

 Find all our assets  

The release 2602 showcases more 130 innovation highlights across the portfolio. Check out our release document for all the details!

We at Cloud ERP and SCM Product Success offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Click on the enablement wheel below and check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you:
